frameworks.name: resting-state functional connectivity
frameworks.one_line: intrinsic correlation between BOLD signals in retinotopically defined sub-regions

frameworks.name: partial correlation analysis
frameworks.one_line: isolates direct pairwise coupling by regressing out shared network effects

frameworks.name: retinotopic mapping
frameworks.one_line: subdivides V1/V2/V3 by quadrant and eccentricity for fine-grained ROI definition

frameworks.name: critical-period plasticity
frameworks.one_line: altered childhood visual experience reshapes adult visual cortex

core_question: Does amblyopia alter short-range retinotopically organized functional connectivity within early visual cortex?
key_assumptions: early visual areas can be meaningfully subdivided by quadrant and eccentricity
partial correlation reveals direct connectivity beyond network-wide effects
resting-state BOLD reflects intrinsic functional architecture
manipulations: ROI scale: sub-area retinotopic patches within V1, V2, V3
connectivity metric: full vs partial correlation
key_findings: only partial (not full) correlation distinguished groups
amblyopes showed higher V1 partial correlation between para-foveal and peripheral representations
this para-foveal-peripheral effect correlated with poorer acuity of the worse eye
amblyopes showed reduced V1-V2 and V2-V3 connectivity between isoeccentric regions
interpretations: abnormal retinotopically organized intra- and inter-area coupling characterizes amblyopic visual cortex
fine-scale partial-correlation analysis reveals deficits invisible to coarse or full-correlation measures
